'''Fodo''' was a [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Musician/Legends|musician]] that played the [[Kloo horn/Legends|kloo horn]] for [[the Twisted Rancor Trio]].

==Biography==
The fifth musician to join the Twisted Rancor Trio, Fodo was hired by the band's manager [[Gilthos Uksaris]] upon the recommendation of [[Loopa]] another Bith that had newly joined the band. Both served as replacements for the previous two Bith brothers [[Ujaa]] and [[Ujii]] who left when the lead singer [[Elinda]] found Ujaa's lackluster music skills intolerable.

This second incarnation of the Twisted Rancor Trio began to rise to fame around the year [[3956 BBY]], being especially popular on its [[Planet/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] as a result of Elinda's voice. The group's big break seemed to have arrived when they were invited to perform for the local businessman [[Davik Kang]] at his [[Davik Kang's estate|estate]]. However, while Uksaris was ecstatic for this opportunity, Elinda objected on the grounds that Kang was heavily rumored to be connected with the crime syndicate the [[Exchange]]. In fears that their lives could be in danger should their performance be unsatisfactory to Kang, Elinda opted out, and Uksaris replaced her with her sister [[Ashana]] on the eve of their scheduled performance.

==Behind the scenes==
Fodo only appears as a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' as a part of a puzzle that the player, [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], can solve in order to crack a security crate booby-trapped with a [[Holo-Sec Corp]] [[Holo-Sec Corp anti-theft explosive|anti-theft explosive]]. The player must activate the six [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojectors]] of the members of the Twisted Rancor Trio surrounding the crate in order of their recruitment. The solution is given in the [[datapad]] lying on a nearby table of Uksaris's ''[[Guts and Glory: A Chronicle of The Amazing Story of The Twisted Rancor Trio]]'' detailing the history of the band.
